title: From 'BuddhaBot' to $1.99 chats with AI Jesus, the faith
description: New artificial intelligence tools are entering the religious space. Companies are creating AI Jesuses, Buddhist priests, and Hindu gurus. These technologies offer prayer and encouragement. They also raise questions about faith, authority, and spiritual guidance. Some see them as helpful tools, while others worry about misinformation and exploitation. The integration of AI into religion is a complex and evolving issue.
content:

title: University undergrads in tech courses to get more career support amid AI disruption
description: A new dedicated job portal lists entry-level positions for cybersecurity, artificial intelligence and data analytics. Read more at straitstimes.com.
content:
SINGAPORE – University undergraduates in tech-related courses will now receive more support to navigate a job market where AI is disrupting many entry-level roles, through a new dedicated job portal that currently has about 800 tech job listings.
The... [4404 chars]

title: Watch: As AI makes more health coverage decisions, the risks to patients grow
description: This year, executives from nearly every major health insurance company made the same declaration in calls with Wall Street analysts: Using artificial intelligence to make coverage decisions would help save them money.
content:
This year, executives from nearly every major health insurance company made the same declaration in calls with Wall Street analysts: Using artificial intelligence to make coverage decisions would help save them money.

title: States must regulate AI because Washington isn't | J.B. Branch
description: Congress has not enacted meaningful artificial intelligence legislation. Yet some in Washington insist the states should be blocked from legislating on AI.
content:
“The main thing is to keep the main thing the main thing,” said Stephen Covey, a renowned organizational consultant.
Branch is the legal counsel for AI governance and technology policy for Public Citizen. He wrote this for InsideSources.com.
